title: Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 Release
description: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.6 on February 5 with 1M token context window, Agent Teams for parallel task execution, Fast Mode with 2.5x faster responses, and 80.8% SWE-bench Verified score. Claude Sonnet 4.6 followed on February 17, positioned at Sonnet-level pricing ($3/$15 per million tokens) with Opus-level coding capabilities and ~75% SWE-bench score.

community_feedback.quote: Claude Opus 4.6 achieves industry-leading 80.8% on SWE-bench Verified benchmark with 1M token context window.
Sonnet 4.6 offers Opus-level coding at Sonnet-level pricing - 'high cost-effectiveness' noted by users.
Agent Teams feature allows multiple AI agents to work in parallel, automatically splitting complex tasks.

title: GPT-5.3-Codex Integration and Model Updates
description: GitHub integrated GPT-5.3-Codex model into Copilot around February 9, 2026, offering 25% performance improvement for agentic coding tasks. Added model selection feature in Copilot Coding Agent via GitHub Mobile v1.245.0. Also deprecated several Anthropic and OpenAI models starting February 17, 2026, requiring explicit administrative enablement for organizational accounts.

community_feedback.quote: Different models have different 'premium request multipliers' affecting usage quotas, requiring administrators to manually enable policies for teams.
GitHub Copilot maintains 68% market share - nearly everyone who uses AI coding tools has used Copilot.
Users noted Claude model神秘消失 from Copilot options, causing confusion among developers.

title: GPT-5.3 Programming Model and macOS Application
description: OpenAI launched macOS version of Codex application and released GPT-5.3 programming model around February 9, 2026. Features include ~245 tokens/second generation speed, CLI integration, image support for screenshots and design mockups, todo list tracking for complex tasks, external system integration with web search and MCP, and improved tool usage accuracy with three approval modes (read-only, automatic, full access).

community_feedback.quote: Described as 'flying fast' - can fix server errors before users finish reading error messages. CLI integration makes it feel 'like a superpower'.
Some users reported completing 2 months of work in just 1 week. ~40% faster for new/greenfield projects.
Occasional struggles with 'big picture' architectural coherence. Doesn't 'think three steps ahead' on architecture.
Some developers use both Opus for architecture and Codex for writing code to balance strengths.

title: Gemini 3 Pro Preview Deprecation Announcement
description: Google announced on February 26, 2026 that Gemini 3 Pro Preview will be deprecated starting March 9, 2026. The '-latest' alias will automatically switch to Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview starting March 6. This API change affects developers using the Gemini CLI and requires migration planning.

community_feedback.quote: Users need to update their applications to use Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview before March 9, 2026 deprecation.

title: Gemini Multi-Step Task Automation on Android
description: Google announced Gemini can now automate multi-step tasks on Android (booking rides, adding items to cart) through 'screen automation' feature on February 25, 2026. Initially limited to US and South Korea on Pixel 10, Pixel 10 Pro, and Samsung Galaxy S26 series. Includes Circle to Search upgrades with multi-object recognition and on-device scam detection.

community_feedback.quote: Users expressed privacy concerns about screen captures during interactions being reviewed by human auditors. Google explicitly advised users NOT to enter login credentials or payment information.
Feature limited to specific devices (Pixel 10/Pro, Galaxy S26) and regions (US, South Korea) at launch.
